channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Mechanical behaviour of additively manufactured metals
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02459-5

The non-equilibrium processing conditions inherent to additive manufacturing produce metals with unique microstructures and mechanical properties that are often unattainable through conventional routes. This Review provides an overview of distinctive mechanical behaviours and deformation mechanisms that arise from these characteristic microstructures, and discusses critical needs and emerging opportunities for alloy design and processing control to achieve exceptional mechanical properties in additively manufactured metals.
item.dc:title: Mechanical behaviour of additively manufactured metals
item.dc:creator: Ting Zhu
Wen Chen

item.title: Large-area non-stoichiometric phase transition in transition metal chalcogenide films
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02471-9

Controllable and scalable phase transition of transition metal chalcogenides is challenging. Using in situ microscopic analysis, a non-stoichiometric phase transition from PdTe2 to PdTe is observed on the atomic scale, providing mechanistic insights into the scalable phase engineering of transition metal chalcogenide films and heterostructures.
item.dc:title: Large-area non-stoichiometric phase transition in transition metal chalcogenide films
item.dc:creator: Zhongqiang Chen

item.title: Heterogeneous doping via nanoscale coating impacts the mechanics of Li intrusion in brittle solid electrolytes
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02465-7

Short-circuiting during fast charging through lithium dendrite intrusion into electrolytes is a major challenge in solid-state batteries. Here, using thermally annealed 3-nm-thick Ag coatings, lithium penetration into brittle electrolyte Li6.6La3Zr1.6Ta0.4O12 is inhibited at local current densities of 250 mA cm−2 due to an increase in surface fracture toughness.
item.dc:title: Heterogeneous doping via nanoscale coating impacts the mechanics of Li intrusion in brittle solid electrolytes
item.dc:creator: Xin Xu

item.title: Quasi-non-volatile capacitorless DRAM based on ultralow-leakage edge-contact MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 14 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02470-w

An in situ multistep edge-contact process is developed for MoS2 transistors, achieving quasi-non-volatile capacitorless two-transistor dynamic random-access memory with 5-bit accuracy and nanosecond write speeds.
item.dc:title: Quasi-non-volatile capacitorless DRAM based on ultralow-leakage edge-contact MoS<sub>2</sub> transistors

item.title: Nonlinear phase-matched van der Waals crystals integrated on optical fibres
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02461-x

Integration of twist-phase-matched van der Waals flakes on optical fibre ends enables efficient nonlinear optical processes, including second-harmonic generation and parametric downconversion, and the fabrication of a frequency-doubling ultrafast laser.
item.dc:title: Nonlinear phase-matched van der Waals crystals integrated on optical fibres
item.dc:creator: Kaifeng Lin
Guangjie Yao
Jiahui Shao

Hao Hong
Kaihui Liu

item.title: Designing heterostructured materials
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 13 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02444-y

Heterostructured materials can exhibit superior performance compared with their homogeneous counterparts. This Perspective discusses the fundamental effects of heterostructural features, methods for evaluating these effects and practical considerations for guiding the design of heterostructured materials.
item.dc:title: Designing heterostructured materials
item.dc:creator: Hao Zhou
Xiaolei Wu
David Srolovitz
Yuntian Zhu
